raw: ADVANTAGES Halfway between skills and powers, advantages are minor benefits characters have, allowing them to do things oth- ers cannot.

subject: Advantage

subject: Ability

subject: Ability

subject: Effect
raw: A power may have just one effect or several, and you can apply various modifiers to the effects to change how they work, customizing them to get just the right power.

subject: Ability
raw: Power effects have ranks like abilities do, on a scale from 1 to 20 (sometimes more).

subject: Ability
raw: Unlike abilities, effects do not have ranks of less than 1, since the “average” is not having powers at all!

subject: Check

subject: Ability
raw: ABILITIES Everyone has certain basic abilities:

subject: Character
raw: Stronger characters can lift greater weights, more agile characters have better balance, tougher characters can soak up more damage, and so forth.

subject: Ability
raw: MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS characters have eight basic abilities:

subject: Ability
raw: ABILITY RANKS Each ability has a rank associated with it, based on how above or below average it is.

subject: Ability
raw: Sometimes your rank is used to calculate another value, such as when you use your Agility to determine how good you are at avoiding harm with your reflexes (your Dodge defense).

subject: Ability
raw: peak of human achievement Low superhuman Moderate superhuman High superhuman Very high superhuman Cosmic Although a rank of 7 is defined as “the peak of human achievement” in an ability on the Ability Benchmarks table, a character with an ability rank greater than 7 isn’t necessarily “non-human,” merely superhuman in comparison to ordinary people.

subject: Ability
raw: Many “normal human” characters in the comics have truly superhuman abilities, particularly mental abilities.

subject: Ability
raw: A character can have a superhuman ability rank without necessarily being anything other than an amazingly talented, well-trained human being.

subject: Ability
raw: On the other hand, a not so bright (low Intellect) character may have great deal of common sense (high Awareness).

subject: Ability
raw: The key differences between Enhanced Abilities and normal ability ranks are Enhanced Abilities can be nullified (normal abilities cannot, see Nullify, page 173) and Enhanced Abilities can have power modifiers and be used for power stunts with extra effort (normal abilities cannot, see Extra Effort, page 19).

subject: Ability
raw: Enhanced Abilities and normal abilities have the same cost (2 power points per +1 ability rank).

subject: Ability
raw: Whenever an ability rank changes, all traits associated with the ability change as well.

subject: Ability
raw: Debilitated Intellect, Awareness, or Presence means the hero is unaware and remains so until re- stored to at least a –5 rank in the ability.

subject: Ability
raw: A creature with no Strength is incapable of exerting any physical force, either because it has no physical form (like an incorporeal ghost) or simply can’t move (like a tree).

subject: Ability
raw: A creature with no Stamina has no physical body (like a ghost) or is not a living being (such as a robot or other construct).

subject: Ability
raw: A creature with no Agility is unable to move its body under its own power and has no Dodge de- fense.

subject: Ability
raw: MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS heroes cannot be absent an ability without Gamemaster permission, as it can have significant effects on the character and the game.

subject: Ability
raw: A creature with no Fighting is incapable of making any sort of close attack (but may still be able to launch ranged attacks, if it has Dexterity).

subject: Ability
raw: Anything with no Intellect is immune to mental effects and in- teraction skills and has no Will defense.

subject: Ability
raw: Anything with no Awareness is com- pletely unaware and also has no Presence.

subject: Ability
raw: Objects are immune to mental effects and interaction skills, and have no Inanimate objects have no abilities other than their Toughness.

subject: Ability
raw: Animate, but nonliving, constructs such as ro- bots or zombies have Strength, Agility, and Dexterity, and may have ranks of Awareness and Presence (if aware of their environment or capable of interaction), and Fighting (if able to make close attacks).

subject: Ability
raw: They may have Intellect (if capable of independent thought), but have no Stamina (since they are not living things).

subject: Ability
raw: A hero’s defenses are abilities used to avoid such things, determining the difficulty to affect a hero with an attack, or to make resistance checks against them.

subject: Defense
raw: For more on defenses in general and how you use them, see Chapter 8.

subject: Ability
raw: Your base defense ranks are equal to your ranks in their associated abilities.

subject: Defense
raw: ACTIVE DEFENSES Dodge and Parry defenses require a measure of action to be fully effective.

subject: Defense
raw: DEFENSE CLASS One use of defenses is determining a defense class, or the difficulty class to affect a target with a particular at- tack.

subject: Attack
raw: So hitting a character with a ranged attack goes against Dodge defense, giving the attack a DC of (Dodge + 10).

subject: Defense
raw: Similarly, affecting someone with a mental power goes against Will defense, with a DC of (Will + 10), and so forth.

subject: Ability
raw: RESISTANCE CHECKS Defenses are also used to measure the ability to over- come certain effects, involving a resistance check of the defense plus a die roll against a difficulty class determined by the effect or hazard.

subject: Character
raw: INITIATIVE When things start happening quickly, MUTANTS & MASTER- MINDS characters use their initiative bonuses to determine who goes first.

subject: Ability
raw: Initiative Modifier = Agility + Advantages (Improved Initiative) + Power Modifiers Characters then act in initiative order, from highest to low- est.

subject: Attack
raw: Whether or not the hero is successful in stopping the trap depends on the outcome of the attack against the bad guy.

subject: Check
raw: For detailed examples of how to use checks in the game and their effects, see the Action & Adventure chapter.

subject: Check
raw: DIFFICULTY CLASS Checks are made against a difficulty class or DC, a num- ber set by the GM, which your check must equal or exceed to achieve success.

subject: Character

subject: Character
raw: If a character’s routine check result is not up to a task, the player still has the option to roll the die, since the task is by definition not routine for that character.

subject: Skill
raw: In MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS, they do so through the use of various skills, described in this chapter.

subject: Skill
raw: Each skill has a rank, used as a bonus to the die roll when using the skill.

subject: Ability
raw: d20 + skill rank + ability modifier + miscellaneous modifiers SKILL RANK CRITICAL SUCCESS Your rank in a skill, based on the points you have invested in that skill.

subject: Ability

subject: Ability
raw: If you use a skill untrained, the ability modifier still applies to the skill check.

subject: Advantage
raw: MISCELLANEOUS MODIFIERS Miscellaneous modifiers to skill checks include modifiers for circumstances, and bonuses from advantages or pow- ers, among others.

subject: Advantage
raw: Those with the right combinations of skills and advantages can even hold their own against super-powered opponents.

subject: Check
raw: When you use a skill, make a skill check to see how you do.

subject: Skill
raw: Based on the circumstances, your result must match or beat a particular number to use the skill successfully.

subject: Check
raw: (See Checks, page 12, for more information.) UNTRAINED SKILL CHECKS Generally, if you attempt a task requiring a skill you don’t have, you make a skill check as normal.

subject: Skill
raw: Attempts to use these skills untrained automatically fail.

subject: Skill
raw: Since interaction skills are intended for dealing with others so- cially, they have certain requirements.

subject: Check
raw: If they can’t hear or understand you for some reason, you have a –5 circumstance penalty to your skill check (see Circumstance Modifiers in The Basics).

subject: Skill
raw: So, you’ll find climbing isn’t its own skill, but is listed as part of Athletics, while bluffing and search are under Deception and Investiga- tion, respectively.

subject: Effect
raw: The GM decides if a particular use of an interaction skill is effective against a group, and may apply modifiers depending on the situation.

subject: Check

subject: Skill
raw: MANIPULATION SKILLS Some skills, called manipulation skills, require a degree of fine physical manipulation.

subject: Ability
raw: If your physical ma- nipulation capabilities are impaired in some fashion (such as having your hands tied or full use of only one hand), the GM may impose a circumstance modifier based on the severity of the impairment.

subject: Ability
raw: Characters lacking the ability to use manipulation skills can still have ranks in them and use them to oversee or assist the work of others (see Team Checks, page 16).

subject: Character
raw: TALENT In game terms there’s no difference between a character who has ranks in a skill because of extensive training and an- other whose skill ranks represent a natural talent or aptitude for the skill.

subject: Character
raw: For example, one character might have a high Persuasion skill based on the character’s extensive training in negotiation, debate, and management.

subject: Character
raw: Another character’s Persuasion skill may stem more from personal attractiveness or a knack for getting others to cooperate, while a third character may have a combination of the two.

subject: Character
raw: LIFE SKILLS When allocating skill ranks for your character consider not just the character’s role as a hero but also the various other skills the character may have picked up in day-to-day life.

subject: Skill
raw: For example, most adults have some sort of Expertise skill as their occupation with at least 3 to 5 ranks (more if they’re especially good at their job).

subject: Character

subject: Equipment
